title: Created cleanappbase-onnx-ios native iOS base app
content: Started a native iOS base application template for web apps with basic ONNX inference support on mobile. Includes ODR (On-Device Resources) for asset distribution and ATT (App Tracking Transparency) integration. Currently working on basic inference with Whisper integration. Provides a foundation for porting existing Android apps to iOS with AI capabilities.

title: Maker Devlog #3: babysitting agents for ONNX inference in iOS
content: Third devlog entry documenting the journey of porting Android apps to iOS, focusing on running ONNX inference on iOS. Exploring the challenges of babysitting AI agents for inference tasks and the build process for iOS.

title: Designed 3D printed magnetic phone holder for monitor
content: Designed and printed a magnetic smartphone holder to mount on top of a computer monitor for filming YouTube Shorts and IG Stories. The holder supports monitors up to 11mm thick and uses 20mm x 3mm center hollow magnets with a metal cover insert. Published the Fusion 360 source file (.f3d) and STL print file on GitHub.

title: Published first devlog and started iOS port journey
content: Published the first version of this devlog website - a simple yet functional platform to track and share progress on various projects. Today's accomplishments: published calorie-clip ONNX models, worked on iOS native layer for porting Android apps (LiteEdit and Edit By Words), and designed, printed, and assembled a monitor support arm to record videos for the channel.

title: Exported calorie-clip model to ONNX with fp32 and fp16 variants
content: Successfully exported Calorie Clip model to ONNX format for optimized inference. Testing revealed that only fp32 and fp16 ONNX variants maintain performance close to the original model. Created comprehensive export pipeline (export_onnx.py), quantization script (quantize_onnx.py), and stress test/comparison script (final_comparison.py) for benchmarking. Published fp32 (354MB) and fp16 (178MB) ONNX models on HuggingFace.

title: Trained Spanish LoRA for pocket-tts with ONNX export
content: Trained a LoRA adapter for Spanish language on Kyutai's pocket-tts model using a 3060 12GB VRAM GPU for approximately one day. The LoRA was then merged into the original model weights. Also added comprehensive documentation including AGENTS.md for AI agents, CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md, and detailed docs for all CLI commands (generate, serve, export-voice) and Python API. Additionally included ONNX export pipeline for optimized inference.

title: Released pocket-tts V2 with Spanish streaming support
content: Published V2 of the Spanish text-to-speech model on HuggingFace with full streaming support for real-time audio generation. Includes ONNX exported models and quantized variants for faster CPU inference. The model is based on Kyutai's pocket-tts architecture optimized for CPU inference.

title: Launched Edit By Words - AI video editor by transcript
content: Published Edit By Words on Google Play Store - a revolutionary video editor that lets you edit footage by simply editing the transcript. Powered by ONNX Whisper integration for on-device transcription and FFmpeg Native for Android video processing. All transcriptions and edits happen locally on the device for privacy. Delete a word in the text, and it's instantly cut from the video. Supports cutting, copying, removing, and moving text segments with automatic video rearrangement.

title: Launched LiteEdit - All-in-one video editor on Android
content: Published LiteEdit on Google Play Store - the ultimate all-in-one video toolkit for creators. Features include trim, crop, resize with aspect ratio presets (16:9, 9:16, 1:1), speed control, video conversion, GIF making, audio extraction, audio mixing, AI subtitles, filters, and watermarks. Built with FFmpeg Native for Android. All processing happens on-device for privacy and fast performance.
